# Time Series Analysis L2

> Build and evaluate time-series models — linear and log-linear trends, AR(p), random walks with unit-root tests, MA, seasonal models, ARMA, and ARCH for conditional volatility

**Category:** Trading · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** quant · time-series · ar-model · random-walk · arch · seasonality · cfa-level-2

## What this does

Build and evaluate time-series models — linear and log-linear trends, AR(p), random walks with unit-root tests, MA, seasonal models, ARMA, and ARCH for conditional volatility

Specifies 2 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **model_id** *(text, required)* — Model identifier
- **model_type** *(select, required)* — linear_trend | log_linear_trend | ar | random_walk | ma | arma | arch | seasonal

## What must be true

- **trend_models → linear:** y_t = b0 + b1*t + e_t; constant arithmetic change
- **trend_models → log_linear:** ln(y_t) = b0 + b1*t + e_t; constant proportional growth rate
- **trend_models → test_for_serial_correlation:** Durbin-Watson on residuals; if violated switch to AR model
- **covariance_stationary → requirements:** Constant mean, constant variance, constant covariance with lagged values
- **covariance_stationary → detect_violation:** Augmented Dickey-Fuller (ADF) unit-root test; H0 = nonstationary
- **ar_models → ar1:** x_t = b0 + b1*x_{t-1} + e_t
- **ar_models → mean_reversion:** Unconditional mean = b0/(1-b1) when |b1| < 1
- **ar_models → multiperiod_chain_rule:** Iterate one-period forecasts forward
- **ar_models → detection_serial_correlation:** t-test on residual autocorrelations; |t| > 2 flags problem
- **random_walks → pure_random_walk:** x_t = x_{t-1} + e_t; no constant or trend
- **random_walks → random_walk_with_drift:** x_t = b0 + x_{t-1} + e_t
- **random_walks → unit_root:** Coefficient on lagged level equals 1; ADF test
- **random_walks → correction:** First-difference the series before regression
- **moving_average → simple_ma:** Average of last m periods used for smoothing/forecasting
- **moving_average → ma_q:** x_t = e_t + θ1*e_{t-1} + … + θq*e_{t-q}
- **seasonality → detection:** Significant autocorrelation at lag k where k = seasonality period (4 quarterly, 12 monthly)
- **seasonality → correction:** Add seasonal AR term x_{t-k} or seasonal dummy variables
- **arch_models → arch_1:** var(e_t) = a0 + a1*e_{t-1}²
- **arch_models → detection:** Regress squared residuals on their lags; significant coefficients indicate ARCH
- **arch_models → use:** Forecast time-varying volatility for risk management
- **forecasting_steps → plot_data:** Inspect for trend, seasonality, structural breaks
- **forecasting_steps → test_stationarity:** ADF; difference if needed
- **forecasting_steps → fit_candidate_model:** Compare AIC/BIC across AR(p)/ARMA(p,q)
- **forecasting_steps → diagnose_residuals:** Test for autocorrelation, ARCH, normality
- **forecasting_steps → out_of_sample_validation:** RMSE on holdout period
- **validation → model_required:** model_id present
- **validation → valid_model_type:** model_type in allowed set

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Fit Time Series** — when model_id exists; model_type in ["linear_trend","log_linear_trend","ar","random_walk","ma","arma","arch","seasonal"], then call service; emit ts.fitted. _Why: Fit time-series model._

**❌ Failure paths**

- **Invalid Model Type** — when model_type not_in ["linear_trend","log_linear_trend","ar","random_walk","ma","arma","arch","seasonal"], then emit ts.fit_rejected. _Why: Unsupported time-series model._ *(error: `TS_INVALID_MODEL_TYPE`)*

## Errors it can return

- `TS_INVALID_MODEL_TYPE` — model_type must be one of the supported time-series models

## Events

**`ts.fitted`**
  Payload: `model_id`, `model_type`, `coefficients`, `aic`, `bic`, `residual_diagnostics`

**`ts.fit_rejected`**
  Payload: `model_id`, `reason_code`

## Connects to

- **multiple-regression-basics-l2** *(required)*
- **regression-misspecification-l2** *(recommended)*
